data.title: Italy data protection agency fines Intesa Sanpaolo $36 million over data breach
data.description:
MILAN, March 30 (Reuters) - Italy's data protection authority said on Monday it had fined the country's biggest bank Intesa Sanpaolo 31.8 million euro ($36.41 million) over a data breach case that involved some 3,500 customers over two years.

($1 = 0.8734 euros)

(Reporting by Elvira Pollina, editing by Cristina Carlevaro)

data.title: Sirnaomics says chairman purchases 38,000 shares at average price HK$5.93 per share
Kam Hing International Holdings posts FY revenue HKD 3,685.9 million
ICE Midday : Crude Oil Lifts Canola, Vegetable Oils
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange rose by double digits in the middle of Monday trading, mirroring the direction taken by comparable oils.

An analyst said that May canola could encounter resistance at C$740 per tonne, adding that Houthi rebels entering the war in Iran will prolong the conflict and raise oil prices further. The analyst also warned of drier weather in Indonesia and Malaysia in the coming months, which would restrict palm oil production.

Crude oil gained more than US$2 per barrel after United States President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iranian energy infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz is not re-opened. In turn, Chicago soyoil, European rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were higher with the spillover pushing up canola.

The Canadian Grain Commission reported 195,000 tonnes of canola were exported during the week ended March 22, compared to 292,100 tonnes the previous week. So far this marketing year, 5.074 million tonnes were shipped versus 6.632 million one year ago.

The Canadian dollar was down two-tenths of a U.S. cent compared to Friday's close.

About 36,900 canola contracts have traded at 10:07 CDT. Prices in Canadian dollars per metric tonne: 


 
           Price      Change 
May       733.80    up 13.30 
Jul       746.90    up 13.60 
Nov       740.60    up 12.70 
Jan       746.90    up 12.40

Source: Commodity News Service Canada, news@marketsfarm.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-30-26 1145ET

data.title: Fed watching private credit sector for signs of trouble, Powell says
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 30 (Reuters) - Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said on Monday the U.S. central bank is watching developments in the private credit sector for signs of trouble, but does not currently see issues there bringing down the financial system as a whole.

"I'm reluctant to say anything that suggests that we're dismissive of the risk," Powell said during an event at Harvard University. "We're looking for connections to the banking system and things that might, you know, result in contagion. We don't see those right now," he said.

As for some of the issues now faced by the opaque banking sector, "there'll be people losing money and things like that, but it doesn't, it doesn't seem to have the makings of a broader systemic event," Powell said.

He noted that when it comes to private credit, "it's a relatively small part of a very large asset pool, we're watching it super carefully," and that regulators are on the issue.

"We're also getting the back story from the people who run these organizations and from all the banks," Powell said, adding that regulators "are well aware of what the banks' exposure is."

(Reporting by Michael S. Derby; Editing by Paul Simao)

By Michael S. Derby

Eni: Entire Itabus fleet now powered by Enilive HVO diesel
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Eni Spa has announced that its partnership with Itabus, initiated in 2021, has reached full scale with the entire bus fleet now operating on Enilive's HVOlution diesel, a biofuel produced from 100% renewable raw materials.

In 2025, Itabus vehicles covered approximately 20 million kilometers using 5.7 million liters of Enilive fuel, of which 3 million liters were HVOlution. This represents an average biofuel utilization rate of 53%, though the trend is accelerating: in December of last year alone, the company reported that HVO accounted for 75% of the fleet's refueling requirements.

In 2026, the average share of pure biofuel refueling is expected to increase further, underscoring the joint commitment of Itabus and Enilive toward progressively more sustainable mobility.

Eni shares closed Monday up 2.6% at EUR24.56 per share.

By Michele Cirulli, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Huatai Securities Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Huatai Securities Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported revenue was CNY 35,809.92 million compared to CNY 33,519.17 million a year ago. Net income was CNY 16,383.5 million compared to CNY 15,351.16 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.73 compared to CNY 1.62 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 1.73 compared to CNY 1.62 a year ago.

data.title: Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was CNY 125,958.7 million compared to CNY 115,456.18 million a year ago. Revenue was CNY 126,678.59 million compared to CNY 116,186.12 million a year ago.

Net income was CNY 1,206.22 million compared to CNY 752.48 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.078 compared to CNY 0.048 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was CNY 0.078 compared to CNY 0.048 a year ago.

India's Bharti Airtel announces $1 billion fund raise for data center business Nxtra
data.description:
March 30 (Reuters) - India's Bharti Airtel said on Monday its data center business, Nxtra Data, has raised $1 billion from Alpha Wave Global, Carlyle and Anchorage Capital.

The funding values Nxtra at $3.1 billion, the company said in a press release.

(Reporting by Nandan Mandayam in Bengaluru; Editing by Tasim Zahid)

Edwards Lifesciences Announces New Data on EVOQUE Transcatheter Tricuspid Valve Replacement System
data.description:
Edwards Lifesciences announced new data on the EVOQUE transcatheter tricuspid valve replacement (TTVR) system at the American College of Cardiology Annual Scientific Session (ACC.26), demonstrating significant and sustained patient benefits including lower mortality when accounting for patient crossover, extending the findings presented at the European Society of Cardiology Congress (ESC 2025). The new TRISCEND II trial data presented during a late-breaking featured research session at ACC.26 demonstrated confidence in two-year performance of TTVR with the EVOQUE system and showed: Significant and sustained near elimination of tricuspid regurgitation (TR); Improvements in health status and quality of life; No added device-related risk; and Significantly lower all-cause mortality when accounting for patient crossover. The 18-month data of the TRISCEND II trial were presented at ESC in August 2025, showing achievement of a hard endpoint benefit for the most severe TR patients who received the EVOQUE therapy, and superior quality of life benefits, regardless of baseline TR.

In addition to TRISCEND I and II data, the growing body of evidence on EVOQUE includes data on more than 1,000 patients in the STS/ACC TVT Registry presented at TCT last year, demonstrating consistent near elimination of tricuspid regurgitation, improved quality of life, and a positive real-world safety profile across the broad tricuspid patient population. The EVOQUE system is approved in both the US and Europe.

data.title: Shenzhen Han's CNC Technology posts FY net profit attributable RMB 824.3 million
Canada Awards C$1.5 Billion in Contracts to Support New CC-330 Tanker Fleet to L3Harris, Airbus
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese


The Canadian government has awarded 1.5 billion Canadian dollars ($1.08 billion) in contracts to keep Canada's new CC-330 Husky tanker fleet operational as part of a broader push to modernize the Royal Canadian Air Force.

The Defence Investment Agency, a new special operating agency created to modernize and speed up defense procurement, has awarded two contracts to L3Harris MAS and one to Airbus to keep the CC?330 Husky fleet ready for service and bolster Canada's aerospace and defense sector.

The CC-330 Husky, based off of the Airbus A330 multi-role tanker transport, is the Royal Canadian Air Force's new long?range tanker and transport aircraft which provides long-range air-to-air refuelling for Canadian and allied aircraft, while also serving as a strategic transport platform for troops, cargo and medical evacuations.

The investments contribute to Canada reaching NATO's defense spending target. After years of falling short of the alliance's 2% requirement, Ottawa now says it has met the goal, helped in part by major procurement programs such as the CC-330 Husky fleet.

The Defence Investment Agency said the three contracts directly could create up to 720 jobs in the provinces of Quebec, Ontario and Alberta, as well as contribute C$90 million annually to Canada's gross domestic product.

The investments are part of Canada's broader push to rebuild its defense industrial base, supporting domestic aerospace growth and ensuring the country can sustain key military capabilities while creating high-skilled jobs domestically.


Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-30-26 1134ET
